body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.75-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-29937/folder/13176687/107348969/1418201973?sort=%2Bidentifier&p=3 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** N/A **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Honor x6b/ Android 16 Windows 10 / chrome **App Component:** User Settings ## Action Performed: Precondition: User has at least two contact methods 1. Navigate to Account > Profile > Contact methods 2. Click on a secondary (non-default) contact method 3. Click "Set as default" 4. Enter an incorrect magic code Result: There is not error displayed indicating the code is invalid and the change is applied 5. Try setting as default another contact method with and incorrect magic code Result: There is not error displayed indicating the code is invalid and the change is applied ## Expected Result: An error is displayed indicating the code is invalid and the change is not applied ## Actual Result: There is not error displayed indicating the code is invalid and the change is applied ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/59880a9a-18a3-4fdc-b0bd-efc6d9f72dca https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/92b5b25a-5f88-44ac-8ba2-8c0c6d6c07b8 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Odometer distance expenses to self-DM lose start/end readings

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Improvement
labels.description: Item broken or needs improvement.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
### Problem When you create an Odometer request to yourself, we don't show the start/end readings in the merchant field: **Expected:** **Actual:** When creating an odometer distance expense to an unreported/self-DM report, `odometerStart` and `odometerEnd` are not forwarded to the backend. The `requestMoney` function in `TrackExpense.ts` (called when `isUnreported=true`) never destructures or includes these fields in its `REQUEST_MONEY` API params, so the server stores no odometer NVPs and the merchant string omits the readings. ### Solution Add `odometerStart` and `odometerEnd` to the transactionParams forwarded through `requestMoney` in `TrackExpense.ts` (lines 1610–1850), or redirect unreported odometer distance expenses through the `createDistanceRequest` path (which already passes these fields correctly).
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @DylanDylann

  • Upwork Job URL: https://www.upwork.com/jobs/~022055436722541765277
  • Upwork Job ID: 2055436722541765277
  • Last Price Increase: 2026-05-15

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.74-6 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-29869/folder/13176744/41237167/1417454241?sort=%2Bidentifier **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** n/a **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Mac 15.4.1 Safari, iPhone 13 iOS 26.3.1 **App Component:** Workspace Settings ## Action Performed: Preconditions: Distance rates are enabled in More features and at leas one custom created distance rate is created 1. Go to https://staging.new.expensify.com 2. Go to the workspace chat 3. Create a map distance request with a custom created distance rate (not the default distance rate) 4. Navigate to the workspace settings and delete the distance rate that was selected when creating the above expense 5. Go to Inbox ## Expected Result: After deleting the distance rate that was used to create the expense, an RBR should be displayed in the workspace chat, and the ‘Rate not valid for this workspace’ error should be displayed on the expense preview. ## Actual Result: After deleting a distance rate, the RBR in the workspace chat and the error on the expense preview appear only after opening the expense details. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/24916d74-68d5-43e6-ba17-ce89cdf49852 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
